steelman_no_trade.draft: A rational Senior could pass because the market may already price in durable SaaS growth and margin expansion, while enterprise software competition and AI platform shifts can pressure renewal and expansion assumptions.
steelman_no_trade.source_label: C-4 DCF scenarios and CRM filed spine
steelman_no_trade.excerpt_or_summary: The filed DCF scenario set and spine identify a profitable SaaS company with valuation sensitivity to growth, margins, sales-to-capital, and WACC.

counterparty.draft: The plausible counterparty is a quality-growth holder reducing exposure because the stock already discounts sustained SaaS base rates while software budget cycles and AI disruption remain hard to underwrite.
counterparty.source_label: C-1 Business and C-4 DCF scenarios
counterparty.excerpt_or_summary: Business and scenario artifacts identify renewal durability, expansion, and margin discipline as the central disagreement mechanism.

draft.mechanism: Investors can underweight the durability of embedded enterprise workflows and multi-cloud expansion because near-term software spending cycles obscure long-lived switching costs.
draft.persistence_reason: The disagreement can persist until several reporting periods show whether subscription revenue growth, renewal, and margins track the SaaS base-rate path.

structural_mispricing.source_label: C-2 Moat and C-4 DCF scenarios
structural_mispricing.excerpt_or_summary: Moat and scenario drafts support switching costs and SaaS margin durability as the main variant-view drivers.

variant_view.draft: The variant view is that the market is underweighting Salesforce's embedded enterprise workflow position and operating leverage while over-indexing on near-term software budget caution and AI disruption headlines.
variant_view.source_label: C-1 Business, C-2 Moat, and C-4 DCF scenarios
variant_view.excerpt_or_summary: Filed Analyst artifacts support a profitable SaaS DCF route rather than a generic cash-generator default.

draft.event: Quarterly subscription revenue growth, remaining performance obligation, and operating margin disclosures
draft.why_it_matters: Tests whether SaaS growth and margin durability track the sector-calibrated DCF assumptions.

draft.event: Data, AI, and platform product attach disclosures
draft.why_it_matters: Tests whether new products reinforce retention and expansion rather than only replacing maturing clouds.

catalysts.source_label: C-4 DCF scenarios and C-6 risk setup
catalysts.excerpt_or_summary: Scenario and risk artifacts identify subscription growth, margin durability, sales-to-capital, and platform attach as thesis drivers.

cruxes.claim: Subscription revenue growth must remain consistent with the SaaS sector base-rate path.
cruxes.threshold_value: growth remains near or above the filed SaaS base scenario path

cruxes.claim: Operating margin must not give back SaaS scale economics.
cruxes.threshold_value: margin remains within the filed SaaS bear-to-base range

cruxes.claim: Platform expansion must offset software competition and AI disruption.
cruxes.threshold_value: disclosures support continued customer expansion and product attach

source_evidence_summary.business: Business artifact frames CRM as a subscription-heavy enterprise cloud software platform.
source_evidence_summary.moat: Moat artifact supports switching costs and platform breadth while flagging competition and AI disruption.
source_evidence_summary.capalloc: Capital allocation artifact supplies reinvestment, dilution, and balance-sheet flexibility context.
source_evidence_summary.scenarios: Scenario artifact identifies SaaS-sector DCF growth, margin, sales-to-capital, and WACC drivers.
source_evidence_summary.mechanical: Gate card, method directive, DCF valuation, reverse DCF, and spine provide mechanical context.
